title: Sticherus cundinamarcensis
intro: Sticherus cundinamarcensis, also known as "Quillwai", is a species of fern belonging to the family Gleicheniaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sticherus cundinamarcensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
